title: Fingolimod -- Mechanism of Action
content: Fingolimod is a nonselective functional antagonist of sphingosine-1-phosphate receptors (S1PRs). [1] Since the emergence of fingolimod in 2010 as the first medication of this class, similar medications with higher receptor specificity have been synthesized and FDA-approved, such as siponimod (2019), ozanimod (2020), and ponesimod (2021). title: Systemic Sclerosis (Scleroderma) -- Treatment / Management -- Specific Therapies in Scleroderma
content: Vasodilator therapy is recommended and may include phosphodiesterase-5 inhibitors (such as tadalafil 40 mg daily and sildenafil 20 mg orally 3 times daily), endothelin receptor antagonists (such as bosentan at 62.5-125 mg twice daily or sitaxsentan, ambrisentan, or macitentan), and/or prostacyclin analogs (such as epoprostenol, treprostinil, beraprost, and iloprost at 2.5-5 μg inhaled 6-9 times daily). Prostacyclin therapy is considered the most effective, although all these agents improve hemodynamics and quality of life. Combination therapy may be considered in patients who fail to improve on monotherapy or have severe PAH.

content: FTY720 (fingolimod) is a novel immunosuppressive agent investigated over the past few decades. FTY has proven highly effective in prolonging graft survival in preclinical models of transplantation and multiple sclerosis. Unlike traditional immunosuppressants, FTY720 is an immunomodulator with a unique mechanism of action, classified as a sphingosine 1-phosphate receptor (S1P-R) modulator. [8] [9] In laboratory animals, activating S1P receptors with fingolimod triggers anti-inflammatory and anti-apoptotic pathways, enhancing myocardial salvage by reducing cardiac fibrosis. [10]

title: Scleroderma-Associated Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension: Early Detection for Better Outcomes (Archived) -- Issues of Concern -- PAH Treatment Algorithm
content: Patients categorized with low-to-intermediate risk are typically treated with combination (or sequential) therapy, although some may be treated with monotherapy; high-risk patients should be treated with combination therapy with IV prostacyclin analogs. [7] Combination therapy is currently recommended for treatment naïve patients with WHO class II (or higher) and has been associated with reduced morbidity and mortality and improved functioning compared with monotherapies. [57] There is evidence of the benefit of monotherapy with ERAs (e.g., bosentan) in some patients (those with WHO classes II and III), but evidence supporting monotherapy with PDE-5 inhibitors is lacking. [6] Combining an ERA with a PDE-5 inhibitor appears to reduce worsening of clinical outcomes compared with ERA monotherapy. Parenteral and inhaled PAs are often reserved for patients with WHO class III and IV, although oral selexipag can be used for patients with WHO class II.

Intravitreal therapy is generally reserved for clinical trials or off-label use in select, non-resolving cases. title: Hairy Cell Leukemia -- Treatment / Management
content: Relapsed disease can be treated with either another course of purine analog (if relapse is more than 1 year from initial treatment); however, response rates are often lower, and remissions shorter after relapse. The BRAF mutation in hairy cell leukemia can be targeted by the oral BRAF inhibitor vemurafenib, which has been shown to be efficacious in both the relapsed and refractory settings.

title: Biochemistry, Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor -- Introduction
content: EGFR and ErbB4 are fully functional receptors that bind ligands and undergo autophosphorylation (ie, tyrosine kinase activation). ErbB2 has no known ligand but is the preferred dimerization partner for EGFR, ErbB3, and ErbB4. ErbB3 has no intrinsic tyrosine kinase activity but can transduce signals through interaction with kinase-active receptors such as EGFR, ErbB2, and ErbB4. Seven known ligands bind and activate EGFRs: EGF, transforming growth factor alpha (TGFa), Amphiregulin (Areg), Betacellulin (Btc), Epiregulin (Ereg), heparin-binding EGF-like growth factor (HB-EGF), and Epigen (Epgn). Tyrosine phosphorylation of receptors stimulates various signaling pathways, including Ras/MAPK, PLCγ1/PKC, PI(3)kinase/Akt, and STAT pathways. Aberrant expression of EGF ligands and hyperactivation of EGFRs due to mutations are implicated in many human diseases ranging from various human cancers to psoriasis, Alzheimer disease, and schizophrenia. The pathologies associated with EGFRs have led to novel treatment modalities, including monoclonal antibodies designed to inhibit EGFR signaling. [1] [2] [3]

title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 1 -- Community 214
content: The community describes blood cancer (ابْيضَاضُ الدَّم) as a group of leukemic disorders comprising lymphoid leukemia (ابيضاض الدم اللمفاوي) and myeloid leukemia (ابيضاض الدم النقوي), which correspond to chronic lymphocytic leukemia (سرطان الخلايا الليمفاوية المزمن) and acute myeloid leukemia (سرطان الدم النخاعي الحاد), respectively. Targeted therapy relies heavily on protein kinase inhibitors, antineoplastic and immunomodulating agents. BCR‑ABL inhibitors—dasatinib, dasatinib anhydrous, imatinib, imatinib mesylát, ponatinib, ponatinib hydrochloride, and asciminib—block the BCR‑ABL tyrosine kinase; the former four are used for lymphoid leukemia, whereas ponatinib, ponatinib hydrochloride, and asciminib are indicated for myeloid leukemia. Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitors ibrutinib and zanubrutinib also belong to this class, with ibrutinib approved for chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Additional protein kinase inhibitors include EGFR inhibitors canertinib dihydrochloride and margetuximab, as well as other ATC‑listed agents cabozantinib, gilteritinib, and midostaurin; gilteritinib and midostaurin are employed in acute myeloid leukemia. Corticosteroids such as dexamethasone acetate (used for blood cancer and septic shock), dexamethasone phosphate, prednisolone sodium metasulphobenzoate, prednisolone sodium phosphate, and prednisolone tebutate demonstrate efficacy in hematologic malignancies. Chemotherapeutic agents irinotecan hydrochloride and pipobroman are also utilized. Contraindicated interventions include infection with human herpesvirus 1 and oncolytic viral therapy talimogene laherparepvec, which may exacerbate the disease. Collectively, these agents illustrate the role of kinase‑targeted and immunomodulatory drugs in the comprehensive management of leukemic disorders.

title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 1 -- Community 81
content: Blood cancer (ابْيضَاضُ الدَّم) encompasses leukemias, including lymphoid (ابيضاض الدم اللمفاوي) and myeloid (ابيضاض الدم النقوي) forms, with specific subtypes such as chronic lymphocytic leukemia (سرطان الخلايا الليمفاوية المزمن) and acute myeloid leukemia (سرطان الدم النخاعي الحاد). Targeted kinase inhibition is a central therapeutic strategy. BCR‑ABL inhibitors—dasatinib, dasatinib anhydrous, imatinib, ponatinib, ponatinib hydrochloride, and asciminib—are employed, the first three for lymphoid leukemia and the latter three for myeloid leukemia. BTK inhibitors, namely ibrutinib and zanubrutinib, belong to the same protein‑kinase inhibitor class, with ibrutinib indicated for chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Additional protein‑kinase inhibitors with antineoplastic and immunomodulating activity include EGFR inhibitors canertinib dihydrochloride and margetuximab, as well as cabozantinib, gilteritinib, and midostaurin; gilteritinib and midostaurin are specifically used in acute myeloid leukemia. Conventional cytotoxic and corticosteroid agents such as cytarabine, dexamethasone acetate, dexamethasone phosphate, dexamethasonisonikotinát, irinotecan hydrochloride, pipobroman, and prednisolone sodium metasulphobenzoate, prednisolone sodium phosphate, and prednisolone tebutate also have activity against various blood cancers. Dexamethasone acetate additionally serves in the management of septic shock. Conversely, viral agents such as human herpesvirus‑1 and the oncolytic therapy talimogene laherparepvec are contraindicated in patients with blood cancer. These interrelated disease classifications, drug classes, and contraindications illustrate the complexity of therapeutic decision‑making in hematologic malignancies.

title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1787
content: MYO ENDO PERICARDIAL & VALVE DISORDERS represent a more specific subset within the broader condition of فشل قلبي (heart failure), indicating that disorders of the myocardium, endocardium, pericardium, and cardiac valves are closely linked to the pathophysiology of heart failure. In clinical practice, the use of fingolimod hydrochlorid, fingolimod lauryl sulfate, and ketamine hydrochloride is contraindicated in patients with فشل قلبي, meaning these medications should be avoided in individuals diagnosed with heart failure to prevent worsening of cardiac function. title: UMLS -- Run 32 -- Level 2 -- Community 1107
content: MYO ENDO PERICARDIAL & VALVE DISORDERS is a broad cardiac disease category that encompasses the more specific condition فشل قلبي (heart failure). فشل قلبي is a key contraindication for several medications: fingolimod, fingolimod hydrochlorid, fingolimod lauryl sulfate, ketamin, and ketamine hydrochloride should not be administered to patients with heart failure because they may worsen the condition. In contrast, flosequinan may be used to treat فشل قلبي, and its therapeutic action includes the physiologic effect of Vasodilation [PE], indicating that flosequinan promotes vasodilation as part of its benefit in heart failure management. This network of relationships underscores the importance of recognizing heart failure both as a specific manifestation within broader myocardial, endocardial, pericardial, and valve disorders and as a critical factor in guiding safe and effective drug therapy.

title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2133
content: Fenylefrin, a potent α‑adrenergic agonist commonly used as a vasopressor, may be employed in the treatment of صدمة إنتانيّة (septic shock). By inducing peripheral vasoconstriction, fenylefrin can help raise systemic vascular resistance and improve arterial blood pressure in patients whose circulatory collapse is driven by overwhelming infection. Its use in septic shock is typically considered when rapid restoration of hemodynamic stability is required, often in conjunction with fluid resuscitation and other supportive measures. Proper dosing and monitoring are essential to balance the benefits of increased perfusion pressure against the risk of excessive vasoconstriction.

title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 2 -- Community 572
content: The community describes a network of ischemic heart disease centered on acute coronary insufficiency (إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ), classified under acute ischemic heart disease, unspecified, and localized to the coronary arteries (věnčité tepny). Vascular ischemia is the parent condition, and the disease gives rise to myocardial, endocardial, pericardial and valve disorders (MYO ENDO PERICARDIAL & VALVE DISORDERS), which in turn are linked to heart failure (فشل قلبي). Specific downstream coronary manifestations include coronary artery spasm (تشنج الشريان التاجي), coronary artery disease (مرض الشريان التاجي), chronic unspecified ischemic heart disease (مرض قلبي إقفاري مزمن، غير محدد), coronary arterial sclerosis (تصلب عصيدي في التاجي), coronary artery inflammation (التهاب الشريان التاجي), and coronary artery rupture. The left coronary artery is composed of branches of the coronary arteries. Therapeutically, antiplatelet agents tirofiban and tirofiban hydrochloride may prevent progression of إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ, whereas fingolimod, fingolimod hydrochloride, fingolimod lauryl sulfate, ketamine and ketamine hydrochloride are contraindicated in patients with إقفار عَضَلِ القَلْبِ or heart failure. Lipid‑lowering and anti‑arteriosclerosis drugs, exemplified by niacinamide, are associated with management of elevated triglycerides (ارتفاع نسبة الدهون الثلاثية في الدم) and may help prevent coronary artery disease. This integrated hierarchy emphasizes the interdependence of vascular ischemia, its cardiac complications, and the importance of appropriate pharmacologic selection in patient care.

title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 1 -- Community 244
content: Heart failure falls within the narrower scope of MYO ENDO PERICARDIAL & VALVE DISORDERS and is linked to congestive cardiomyopathy. Several pharmacologic agents are contraindicated in the setting of heart failure, including cyclobenzaprine, the sphingosine‑1‑phosphate receptor modulators fingolimod, fingolimod hydrochlorid, fingolimod lauryl sulfate, and the anesthetics ketamin and ketamine hydrochloride. Therapeutic management of conduction disorders often involves pacing devices; the procedure “Initial insertion of transvenous lead [electrode] into ventricle” is clinically associated with a kardiostimulátor (pacemaker). Pericardial interventions form a hierarchical chain—procedure in the pericardium (عملية في التأمور) → pericardial biopsy (خزعة التأمور) → pericardial excision (استئصال تأموري) → cardiac valvotomy (استئصال صمام القلب)—situated within the broader operation category “Operation on heart and pericardium” and linked to SMQ, a member of the pericardial procedure hierarchy.

title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 3 -- Community 1792
content: MYO ENDO PERICARDIAL & VALVE DISORDERS are closely linked to فشل قلبي (heart failure), indicating that these myocardial, endocardial, pericardial, and valve abnormalities often coexist with or contribute to the development of heart failure. In patients with فشل قلبي, several pharmacologic agents must be avoided because they are contraindicated in this condition. Cisapride monohydrate is contraindicated for use in individuals with فشل قلبي, as are the immunomodulatory drug fingolimod and its formulation fingolimod lauryl sulfate. Additionally, ketamine hydrochloride is also contraindicated in the setting of فشل قلبي. Recognizing the relationship between MYO ENDO PERICARDIAL & VALVE DISORDERS and heart failure, and the contraindications of cisapride monohydrate, fingolimod, fingolimod lauryl sulfate, and ketamine hydrochloride, is essential for safe medication selection and optimal management of patients with heart failure.

title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 3 -- Community 2605
content: Myocardial, endocardial, pericardial, and valve disorders (MYO ENDO PERICARDIAL & VALVE DISORDERS) are recognized as a narrower subset of heart failure (فشل قلبي), indicating that these structural cardiac conditions are closely linked to the development or presence of heart failure. Chest constriction (خُنَاقٌ صَدْرِيّ) is also related to heart failure, reflecting a clinical manifestation that may accompany or signal worsening cardiac function. Several pharmacologic agents are contraindicated in the setting of heart failure: fingolimod, fingolimod hydrochlorid, fingolimod lauryl sulfate, and ketamine hydrochloride are all identified as drugs that should not be used in patients with فشل قلبي. Consequently, clinicians managing patients with heart failure should avoid these medications and remain aware of the interconnection between structural heart disorders, chest constriction symptoms, and the need for careful drug selection to optimize patient care.

content: Leukemia, referred to in Arabic as ابْيضَاضُ الدَّم, is the disease under which leukemias are classified. title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 2 -- Community 1138
content: Triamcinolon is classified as a member of the “Corticosteroids, moderately potent, other combinations” group, which in turn is a subclass of the broader “corticosteroids, other combinations” category; this hierarchy also includes “Corticosteroids, potent, other combinations” and “Corticosteroids, weak, other combinations” as sibling subclasses. As a moderately potent corticosteroid, triamcinolon may be used to treat multiple sclerosis (التصلب اللويحي), but it is contraindicated in the context of pregnancy (حالَةٌ رَبْوِيَّة). Hydrocortisone sodium succinate, another corticosteroid formulation, is considered appropriate for use during pregnancy (حالَةٌ رَبْوِيَّة) and is also indicated for the management of circulatory collapse (الانهيار في الدورة الدموية). Understanding these relationships—drug class membership, potency levels, therapeutic indications, and contraindications—helps clinicians select the right corticosteroid for specific conditions while avoiding use in situations such as pregnancy where triamcinolon is unsafe.
